summary: Joel briefly updates listeners about StackOverflow Podcast episode 18, his first conversation with Jeff Atwood since the beta launched. He expresses genuine enthusiasm about the beta's performance, noting it exceeds his highest expectations. The hybrid system combining wiki, Q&A, discussion, and Reddit-like features effectively surfaces the best answers. He highlights that the ability to edit questions and answers creates a continuous improvement loop.
key_points: The StackOverflow beta exceeded even Joel's highest expectations in terms of performance and usability
The hybrid wiki/Q&A/discussion/Reddit model effectively bubbles the right answers to the top
Editability of old questions and answers creates a flywheel where content improves over time
Even with a small beta audience, users were getting great answers quickly
money_quotes: The bizarre wiki/q&a/discussion/reddit hybrid system does a great job of bubbling the right answers to the top.
The ability to edit old questions and answers means that answers just get better and better.
key_insight: StackOverflow's hybrid model combining wiki editability with Q&A, discussion, and voting creates a system where answer quality continuously improves over time.
